files.Title: DotBoxD.Abstractions.CapabilityAttribute
files.Summary: Gates an event property behind a capability. Reading the property from kernel IR contributes id to the manifest's required capabilities, so a kernel that touches the property only installs under a policy granting it. Unannotated properties stay ungated (default-allow).

files.Title: DotBoxD.Abstractions.GeneratePluginRegistrationAccumulatorAttribute
files.Summary: Requests a generated client-side registration accumulator for a control type. The generated accumulator queues calls to methodName and flushes them in order when the plugin builder starts.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Abstractions.GeneratePluginRegistrationRootAccumulatorAttribute
files.Summary: Requests a generated root registration accumulator that exposes child accumulators for annotated child control properties.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Abstractions.GeneratePluginServerAttribute
files.Summary: Requests a generated plugin facade and builder for the annotated partial class.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Abstractions.GeneratedKernelMethodDescriptorAttribute
files.Summary: Analyzer-visible generated IR for server-authored SDK context [KernelMethod] helpers.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Abstractions.GeneratedPluginServerRegistryAttribute
files.Summary: Identifies a generated plugin-server hook or subscription registry and the context type its parameterless On<TEvent> method uses.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Abstractions.HandleSubtypeAttribute
files.Summary: Declares one supported subtype for a handle. The analyzer lowers handle is T local to {BindingPrefix}.is(key), and instance host-binding calls on the declared subtype receive that key as their leading sandbox argument.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Abstractions.HookAttribute
files.Summary:
Associates a hook context type with a stable hook name and exactly one result type, so context.Server.Hooks.On<TContext>() can resolve the result type from the context alone (rather than requiring a second generic argument at the call site). The analyzer reads this attribute to validate that a .Register(...) / .RegisterLocal(...) terminal produces , and persists as the runtime hook-point identity.

The must be a readonly partial record struct decorated with that declares a bool Success and a string? Reason field (see that attribute for the contract).

files.Title: DotBoxD.Abstractions.HookResultAttribute
files.Summary:
Marks a readonly partial record struct as a hook result: a value a .Register(...) / .RegisterLocal(...) terminal returns and the host applies. The DotBoxD generator emits builder members the user did not declare manually — Ok(), Reject(string? reason = null), and a With<Field>(value) per non-discriminator field — so result construction reads fluently and lowers to verified record.new IR.

A hook result must be a top-level readonly partial record struct that declares a bool Success field and a string? Reason field. Success = false means "abstain, fall through to the next matching registration"; a successful result may still carry a domain veto such as CanDie = false, so abstain and veto never overload the same field.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Abstractions.HostBindingAttribute
files.Summary: Marks a host-service method as a sandbox binding the DotBoxD.Kernels generator may call from verified kernel IR. A kernel reaches the service through or through a constructor-injected service field (e.g. _world.GetHealth(id)); the generator lowers that call to a CallExpression(bindingId, …), records capability in the manifest's required capabilities, and adds effects to the manifest's effects. The host registers a matching binding (same id, capability, and effects) so install-time policy and effect validation gate the call. Set when that registered binding declares asynchronous host work.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Abstractions.HostBindingEffect
files.Summary: Explicit host-service effects for analyzer-visible auto bindings.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Abstractions.HostCapabilityAttribute
files.Summary: Declares the capability and host-state effects required by an analyzer-visible host binding contract.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Abstractions.IExtensibleControl
files.Summary: A domain control that can host plugin-owned server extensions.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Abstractions.IHookResult
files.Summary: The runtime contract every hook result satisfies so dispatch can apply the abstain/fallthrough rule without reflecting on the concrete type: Success == false means "abstain, fall through to the next matching registration". The DotBoxD generator adds this interface to every record (its Success field implements the member), so authors never write it by hand.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Abstractions.ILiveSettingsHandle<TKernel>
files.Summary: Strongly typed live-settings tuner for an installed kernel.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Abstractions.IPluginEventValueWriter<TEvent>
files.Summary: Optional low-allocation event adapter path. Implement this when event values can be written directly into the runtime input buffer; must match .

files.Title: DotBoxD.Abstractions.IPluginServer<TWorld>
files.Summary: Lifecycle and anonymous server-side invocation surface mixed into a generated plugin facade.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Abstractions.IServerExtensionClientRegistry
files.Summary: Registry used by generated domain controls to resolve server-extension clients.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Abstractions.IServerExtensionWireClient
files.Summary: Wire client used by generated server-extension proxies.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Abstractions.IServiceControl
files.Summary: The root plugin-service control verbs exposed by a generated plugin facade.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Abstractions.KernelMethodAttribute
files.Summary:
Marks a reusable helper method whose body the DotBoxD.Kernels generator inlines into the kernel/hook IR at every call site, so plugin authors can factor shared gate/handler logic out of a Where/Select/Run lambda (or a kernel-class ShouldHandle/Handle) without leaving the sandbox. The helper can be a static method, or an instance method called on the generated server-context parameter. For example:

server.Hooks.On<MonsterAggroEvent>()
    .Where((e, ctx) => IsBullying(e.MonsterLevel, e.PlayerLevel))
    .Run((e, ctx) => ctx.Messages.Send(e.MonsterId, "calm"));

[KernelMethod]
public static bool IsBullying(int monsterLevel, int playerLevel) => monsterLevel - playerLevel >= 3;

The call lowers exactly as if the body were written inline: each parameter is replaced by its already-lowered argument IR, and any [HostBinding] calls or [Capability]-gated reads inside the body contribute their capabilities to the calling kernel's manifest.

Static helpers may be called through ordinary static-call syntax or extension-method syntax, and named arguments plus supported optional parameter defaults are bound before lowering.

Constraints (verified at generation time; a violation fails the chain/kernel safely rather than miscompiling): the method must be static or called on the server-context parameter, have an expression body or a single return statement, and use types the kernel marshaller can represent in the current lowering surface (scalars, supported nullable scalars, enums, GUIDs, records/DTOs, lists, and maps where that surface supports them). Recursion, generic helpers, ref/out/in parameters, and params arrays are not allowed.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Abstractions.LocalAttribute
files.Summary: Marks a server-authored context helper as native-only and unavailable to lowered IR.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Abstractions.PolymorphicHandleAttribute
files.Summary: Marks a polymorphic host handle type whose sandbox representation is the scalar value stored in the configured key member. Hook contexts may expose the handle type, while generated verified IR receives only the key and calls host bindings to discriminate or query the concrete host-side entity.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Abstractions.RemoteServerInvocation<TWorld, TCaptures, TReturn>
files.Summary: Lambda shape for the explicit capture-bag invoke overload on .

files.Title: DotBoxD.Abstractions.ServerExtensionAttribute
files.Summary:
Marks a class as a server extension: a batch operation the plugin ships as verified IR and the server runs request/response in a single roundtrip, so a loop over many entities executes server-side (calling the host's existing bindings) instead of one network call per entity. The generator lowers the class's single public batch method — its body may use locals, a foreach over a list parameter, host bindings via ctx.Host<T>() or constructor-injected service fields, and may build and return complex objects (records/DTOs) and lists of them. For example:

[ServerExtension("monster-killer", typeof(IMonsterKillerService))]
public sealed partial class MonsterKillerKernel
{
    private readonly IGameWorld _world;
    public MonsterKillerKernel(IGameWorld world) => _world = world;

    public List<KillResult> KillMonsters(List<int> monsterIds, HookContext ctx)
    {
        var results = new List<KillResult>();
        foreach (var id in monsterIds)
            results.Add(new KillResult(id, _world.Kill(id)));
        return results;
    }
}
public readonly record struct KillResult(int MonsterId, bool Success);

The trailing parameter is the lowering marker for host bindings (as in a kernel's Handle) and is not part of the wire signature. Parameters, return type, and DTO fields must use the supported scalar types, lists, or nested DTOs. Supplying the optional service interface type lets the analyzer emit a source-generated plugin-side client that marshals directly to compact server-extension value bytes instead of using a reflection proxy.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Abstractions.ServerExtensionClientAttribute
files.Summary: Requests a generated C# 14 extension property on receiverType that resolves the source-generated server extension client for this service. The receiver type must expose a ServerExtensions property whose value can invoke server extensions and resolve the installed service id.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Abstractions.ServerExtensionMethodAttribute
files.Summary: Requests a generated C# 14 extension method on receiverType that forwards to the source-generated server extension client. When name is omitted, the kernel method name is used; supply a custom name to make the receiver's domain API read naturally or avoid conflicts.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Codecs.MessagePack.MessagePackRpcSerializer
files.Summary: MessagePack-based serializer implementation.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Hosting.ISandboxWorkerClient
files.Summary: Advanced SPI for wiring to a real out-of-process boundary. A consumer implements the transport/process lifecycle and runs the requested plan on the worker side. The host re-validates the returned result envelope before publishing it, so a misbehaving worker fails closed rather than escaping the sandbox contract.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Hosting.SandboxHostWorkerClient
files.Summary: Reference that bridges a worker request to a worker-side . The worker independently re-prepares the plan from the transmitted module and policy instead of trusting the requesting host's signed plan, then executes it with the supplied (in-process) options. When the worker is hosted in a separate process, container, or restricted account, this provides a real out-of-process boundary with a documented envelope.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Kernels.BreakStatement
files.Summary: Exits the innermost enclosing loop (break;).

files.Title: DotBoxD.Kernels.ContinueStatement
files.Summary: Skips to the next iteration of the innermost enclosing loop (continue;).

files.Title: DotBoxD.Kernels.Policies.CapabilityPattern
files.Summary: Hierarchical capability matching for dotted capability ids (e.g. game.world.monster.health.get). A grant may be a wildcard; a requirement is always a concrete id. A grant of game.world.monster. matches any required id under game.world.monster. (at least one further segment); a bare matches everything; otherwise matching is exact.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Kernels.Sandbox.GuidValue
files.Summary: A 128-bit carried as a first-class sandbox scalar. The kernel never operates on it (no arithmetic, comparison, or string semantics) — it only reads, stores, projects, and returns it — so it crosses the wire as a compact 16-byte value rather than its 36-char text form.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Kernels.Sandbox.SandboxInt32Math
files.Summary: Checked 32-bit integer arithmetic with sandbox error semantics. Overflow is detected with branchless bit tests (no closures, no try/catch) so each operation is allocation-free and inlineable on the compiler's unboxed fast path and the interpreter alike. Every overflow / divide-by-zero raises the same error as before.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Kernels.Sandbox.SandboxInt64Math
files.Summary: Checked 64-bit integer arithmetic with sandbox error semantics. Overflow is detected with branchless bit tests for add/subtract and a minimized checked multiply path, so each operation is allocation-free and inlineable on the compiler's unboxed fast path and the interpreter alike — mirroring . Every overflow / divide-by-zero raises the same error.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Kernels.Serialization.Json.Schema.JsonSchemas
files.Summary: Exposes the versioned, machine-readable JSON Schema artifact that describes the public Safe IR module ingestion envelope accepted by . The plugin package envelope schema lives with the plugin layer in DotBoxD.Plugins.PluginPackageJsonSchemas.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Kernels.Verifier.Diagnostics.VerifierDiagnosticCategory
files.Summary: Classifies what a verifier diagnostic most likely indicates, so operators can distinguish a user-fixable generated shape problem from artifact tampering, a host/runtime version mismatch, or a malformed input artifact.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Kernels.Verifier.Diagnostics.VerifierDiagnosticCodes
files.Summary: Public, maintained reference for the V-* diagnostic codes emitted by through . Consumers should reference these constants instead of hard-coding magic strings, and can use / to map a code to its documented meaning, likely cause, category, and remediation.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Kernels.Verifier.Diagnostics.VerifierDiagnosticReference
files.Summary: Public reference entry for a single verifier diagnostic .

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.IServerExtensionClientRegistry
files.Summary: Adds service-contract lookup for generated domain-style server extension client extensions.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.IServerExtensionWireClient
files.Summary: Client-side transport used by generated server extension proxies. The payload is DotBoxD's compact server extension value IR encoded by , so transports can carry it as an ordinary binary IPC argument without knowing the plugin-owned service contract.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.IndexPredicateOperator
files.Summary: The comparison an applies, normalized so the event property is always the left operand (e.Damage >= 5 and 5 <= e.Damage both lower to ).

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.IndexedPredicate
files.Summary: One index-eligible predicate from a lowered .Where(...) chain: a comparison between an event property () and a compile-time constant (). Hosts match against their own indexed fields and dispatch through equality/range buckets.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.Indexing.EventIndexKeyAttribute
files.Summary:
Marks an event property the host keeps a dispatch index for. DotBoxD owns predicate lowering and exposes index metadata on the plugin manifest (); this attribute is how a host declares which of those property paths it can actually serve from an equality/range bucket. reads it (once, with a compiled getter) and ignores manifest predicates whose path is not an index key, leaving them to the verified IR.

Promoted to the framework as the first-class declaration surface (issue #50) so any host can opt into index-based prefiltering without reimplementing the matcher. It stays purely declarative: a property the host does not mark is simply never served from the index.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.Indexing.EventIndexMatcher<TEvent>
files.Summary:
A host's compiled view of a subscription's metadata — the "compile the metadata into whatever dispatch/index structure is natural for the runtime" half of issue #47, promoted to the framework by issue #50. It keeps only the predicates whose is an property of TEvent (the fields the host indexes) and evaluates them cheaply against an event through precompiled property getters — no per-event reflection, so the index never costs more than it saves.

Because every kept predicate is a necessary AND condition of the real predicate, returning false is always a safe reject; returning true means the event passed the cheap index and the host should still run the verified IR unless the manifest reported full coverage. Conversely, the matcher never rejects on a comparison it cannot soundly decide (a value whose type cannot be reconciled to the property, a null reference, or an ordering it cannot evaluate): such a leaf is dropped or passed through so the verified IR stays the authority. This keeps the invariant "index-reject ⇒ verified-IR-reject" intact even for hand-built or imported manifests whose disagrees with the property's CLR type.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.Indexing.EventIndexRegistry
files.Summary:
A first-class, reusable host dispatch index (issue #50). A host registers a subscription kernel together with its generated s; the registry recomputes them from verified IR and compiles them into an (precompiled getters, no per-event reflection) and, when the host publishes an event, runs the cheap index check before entering the sandbox. Events the index rejects never reach the verified IR; survivors are dispatched to as the correctness authority — the verified ShouldHandle still runs after a matching index check because package-supplied coverage metadata is not trusted across the manifest boundary.

This is the "register a subscription and get index-based prefiltering without writing your own matcher" surface. Subscriptions whose predicates touch no indexed field are rejected by (returns false) so the host can leave them on its broad pipeline.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.Indexing.EventIndexStats
files.Summary: Prefilter diagnostics for an : how many indexed checks ran (), how many events the index rejected before any sandbox entry (), and how many survived to the verified IR (). Considered == Prefiltered + Dispatched holds once publishing is quiescent; a snapshot taken while is running concurrently may observe an in-flight increment between the three reads.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.Json.PluginPackageJsonSchemas
files.Summary: Exposes the versioned, machine-readable JSON Schema artifact that describes the plugin package envelope accepted by . The schema is checked into schemas/v1/ and embedded into this assembly so consumers (admin UIs, upload validators, plugin authors) can validate JSON before sending it to a server. The module envelope and the shared live in the purpose-agnostic DotBoxD.Kernels.Serialization.Json package.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.Json.PluginPackageJsonSerializer
files.Summary: The write (export) half of . The read half (with the strict RequireAllowedProperties shape that the schema-sync regression pins) stays in the main file. The optional is emitted only for server extension kernels.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.Kernel.InstalledKernel
files.Summary: Request/response invocation for a server extension kernel: the path that runs a verified batch entrypoint server-side in one call and returns its result. Kept in a partial alongside the event ShouldHandle/Handle path in .

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.Kernel.KernelPackageRegistry
files.Summary: Resolves a kernel CLR type to its analyzer-generated , so a host or a plugin shim can ship a kernel "by type" (e.g. Register<TService, TKernel>()) without naming the generated {Kernel}PluginPackage explicitly.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.KernelRpcBinaryCodec
files.Summary: Encodes server extension IR values as a small binary payload: one value-kind byte followed by only the active scalar or child sequence. The IPC layer transports the resulting bytes as an ordinary binary argument, avoiding reflection-bound DTO maps and repeated string kind tags on the wire.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.KernelRpcValue
files.Summary: A compact intermediate representation for plugin-defined server extension arguments and results. Scalars carry only their active field, and lists/records carry positional child values matching the verified kernel IR type.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.KernelRpcValueConverter
files.Summary: Converts between the compact server extension wire IR and the sandbox values consumed by installed verified IR. The expected sandbox type is supplied by the server-side installed function signature.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.KernelRpcValueKind
files.Summary: The compact, transport-neutral value kinds used for server extension IPC payloads.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.KernelWireKind
files.Summary: How an installed kernel's verified terminal must be wired into a hook or subscription pipeline. This is the single classification the host-side router ( / ) computes once from install-owned + verified metadata, replacing the per-host boolean lattice (is-local-terminal / is-result / is-result-local-terminal) every host used to hand-roll.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.KernelWireTerminal
files.Summary: The trusted terminal classification for an : which pipeline terminal to wire and the arguments it needs. Recomputed from verified IR / install-owned metadata — never trusted from the raw manifest — so a single audited replaces the hand-written routing every host used to copy.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.PluginServer
files.Summary: In-process server-extension convenience surface: register a batch kernel under a service contract, then obtain a typed proxy that invokes it request/response. Plugin-facing remote facades usually expose the same capability from the domain surface they extend.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.PluginSession
files.Summary: Server-side owner of the kernels installed over one connection. Each kernel it installs is tagged with this session as its owner, so another session cannot replace or mutate it. Disposing the session — e.g. from a transport disconnect handler — revokes and unregisters every kernel it owns.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.Runtime.Diagnostics.PluginDiagnosticAudience
files.Summary: Identifies who must act on a runtime plugin diagnostic. Distinguishes a plugin-authoring mistake (fix the plugin source/manifest and re-upload) from a host/operator configuration problem (fix the live setting value or registration the host supplied).

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.Runtime.Diagnostics.PluginDiagnosticCodes
files.Summary: Public, maintained reference for the runtime DBXK* diagnostic codes emitted by the DotBoxD.Plugins package during plugin-package install, prepared-package validation, runtime kernel entrypoint checks, and live-setting validation.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.Runtime.Diagnostics.PluginDiagnosticPhase
files.Summary: The runtime phase that emits a plugin DBXK* diagnostic, so a host can tell a static-package-upload rejection from a prepared-plan mismatch or a live runtime check.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.Runtime.Diagnostics.PluginDiagnosticReference
files.Summary: Public reference entry for a single runtime plugin diagnostic emitted by the DotBoxD.Plugins package.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.Runtime.Hooks.HookStage<TEvent, TCurrent, TContext>
files.Summary: A re-typed stage in a hook chain after a HookPipeline<TEvent>.Select. It carries a composed projection of the original event to the element currently flowing (TCurrent) plus a short-circuit flag, so Where/Select compose without re-keying the pipeline (it stays keyed by TEvent). Terminals re-run the projection per publish and short-circuit when a Where rejected the element. Every fluent method offers both the (element, context) and element-only lambda arities, chosen independently per stage.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.Runtime.Hooks.RemoteLocalHandlerRegistry
files.Summary: Client-side registry for remote RunLocal terminals. A remote server.Hooks.On<TEvent>().Where(..).Select(..).RunLocal(λ) chain lowers only its Where/Select stages to verified IR that filters and projects server-side; the projected value is pushed back over the control-plane callback per passing event. This registry holds the native RunLocal delegate (real host C#, never lowered), keyed by the subscription id returned at install time, and decodes each pushed payload back to the projected CLR type before invoking that delegate.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.Runtime.Hooks.RemoteLocalPush
files.Summary: Delivers an encoded, filtered+projected value to the plugin's native RunLocal delegate across the control-plane callback. The server-side pipeline invokes this once per event that passes the lowered filter; the implementation forwards projectedValue over the IPC boundary keyed by subscriptionId. Transport-agnostic so the pipeline does not depend on the RPC layer.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.Runtime.IErasedPluginEventAdapter
files.Summary:
A type-erased, wire-capable view of a registered . The closure is captured once at registration time (when the event type is statically known), so the host-side router ( / ) can wire an installed kernel to the correct typed pipeline terminal by event name — with no reflection at wire time.

Public as a composability seam: resolve one via and wire a kernel your own way when the / defaults don't fit — without dropping all the way back to a hand-written event-name → typed-On<TEvent>() dispatch.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.Runtime.RemoteHookRegistry
files.Summary: Client-side hook registration surface for a remote plugin server. The fluent stages are lowered by the analyzer; a Run terminal installs the generated verified-IR package through the supplied control-plane callback, while a RunLocal terminal additionally registers its native delegate in the supplied local-handler registry so the server can push filtered+projected values back to it.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.Runtime.RemoteSubscriptionRegistry
files.Summary: Client-side fire-and-forget subscription registration surface for a remote plugin server. A RunLocal terminal registers its native delegate in the supplied local-handler registry so the server can push filtered+projected values back to it per matching event.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.Runtime.ResultHookDispatchOptions<TResult>
files.Summary: Per-dispatch options for result-returning hooks. Local handlers keep the existing in-process semantics; the timeout applies only to remote result handlers because they cross an IPC boundary that can stall.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.Runtime.ResultHookFault
files.Summary: A fault caught while dispatching a result-returning hook (.Register(...) / .RegisterLocal(...)) for one hook context type. HookRegistry.FireAsync isolates a faulting handler — it abstains and falls through to the next registration so one bad handler cannot break the hook point — but that isolation is otherwise silent, which would let a veto-bearing handler (a successful result carrying e.g. CanDie = false) that throws fail open to the host default with no trace. The host observes these faults via PluginServer.Create(onResultHookFault: ...) to surface the failure in its log, mirroring for fire-and-forget subscriptions. Control flow is unchanged: a faulted handler is still skipped and dispatch still falls through to the next registration.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.Runtime.Rpc.KernelRpcMarshaller
files.Summary: Marshals between plain C# values and the sandbox world for server extension service calls: caller arguments are converted to sandbox values for , and the returned value is converted back to the declared C# result type. Supports the supported scalars, List<T>/IEnumerable<T>, and DTOs (records/structs/classes) mapped to positional records by their fields' declaration order — the same order the analyzer used when it lowered the kernel, so fields line up by position.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.Runtime.Rpc.ServerExtensionProxy
files.Summary: A runtime proxy that implements a server-extension interface by marshaling each call through an installed batch kernel. Arguments are converted to sandbox values, the verified IR runs request/ response via , and the result is marshaled back to the method's return type, so server.ServerExtension<IMonsterKiller>().KillMonsters(ids) returns real C# objects. The service is expected to declare a single batch method; synchronous, Task<T>, and ValueTask<T> return shapes are supported.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.Runtime.SubscriptionDeliveryFault
files.Summary: A fault caught while delivering a published event to a subscription. Delivery runs on a background task and these faults are otherwise swallowed (so the game loop is never blocked or crashed by plugin code); the host observes them via PluginServer.Create(onSubscriptionFault: ...) to surface the failure in its log, so a misbehaving Subscriptions.On<T>().Where(...).RunLocal(...) chain is diagnosable instead of silently doing nothing.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.Runtime.SubscriptionDeliveryStage
files.Summary: Which stage of subscription delivery threw. is a lowered server-side Where/projection predicate; is a terminal handler (including a RunLocal projection push and the native decode/dispatch it drives across the host boundary).

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.Runtime.Subscriptions.SubscriptionStage<TEvent, TCurrent, TContext>
files.Summary: A re-typed stage in a subscription chain after a SubscriptionPipeline<TEvent>.Select.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.WireCallbacks
files.Summary: The remote-callback delegates a projecting terminal needs: for a remote RunLocal chain and for a remote RegisterLocal chain. Either may be null for a connection that uses no remote-local terminal; the router only requires the one a kernel's classified terminal actually uses.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.WireOptions
files.Summary: The host's wiring seam for / . Everything mechanical (terminal selection, by-name event resolution, the trusted recompute) is owned by the router; this carries only the genuinely host-specific bits: the remote-local callbacks, the world-owned event index, and an optional classification override.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Plugins.WireResult
files.Summary: The resolved event adapter and terminal used when an installed kernel is wired through or .

files.Title: DotBoxD.Pushdown.Services.PluginConnectionHost<TConnection>
files.Summary:
Accepts ONE plugin connection and owns the per-connection trust-boundary lifecycle: it listens for the connecting peer, mints a for it, runs configure to provision that peer (provide host services over the session and return the per-connection object callers await), and disposes the session — revoking and unregistering the kernels that peer owned — when the peer disconnects OR the host is stopped/disposed.

This is the per-connection IPC ceremony every plugin host used to hand-write. Forgetting the dispose-on-disconnect step silently leaks a peer's kernels, so the framework owns it; the host is left with only the genuinely connection-specific work of choosing which services to provide. The helper is opt-in: everything it does is reproducible with public API ( / / peer.Provide / peer.Disconnected).

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Attributes.DotBoxDMethodAttribute
files.Summary: Marks a method as a DotBoxD endpoint. This attribute is optional - all methods in a [DotBoxDService] interface are included by default. Use this attribute to customize method behavior.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Attributes.DotBoxDServiceAttribute
files.Summary: Marks an interface as a DotBoxD service. The source generator will create client proxy and server dispatcher implementations for this interface.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Buffers.Payload
files.Summary: A single-owner buffer rented from . Dispose returns the backing array to the pool exactly once. Treat as read-only after the buffer has been handed off (it is mutable only to support the receive fill path and the contract).

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Buffers.PooledBufferWriter
files.Summary: An backed by an array rented from . Either hand the written bytes off via or release them via — never both.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Diagnostics.RpcDiagnosticErrorEventArgs
files.Summary: Describes an internal DotBoxD diagnostic error that was observed but not allowed to interrupt RPC teardown or event dispatch.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Diagnostics.RpcDiagnostics
files.Summary: Central diagnostic hooks for errors DotBoxD observes on best-effort paths.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Diagnostics.RpcDisconnectedEventArgs
files.Summary: Describes why a 's read loop ended.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Diagnostics.RpcDispatchErrorEventArgs
files.Summary: Describes an inbound request dispatch or response-send failure.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Diagnostics.RpcErrorInfo
files.Summary: The error returned to a remote caller for a handler exception, produced by on the side that runs the service. becomes the caller's RemoteServiceException message and its remote error-type name (e.g. a value from or a custom application error code).

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Diagnostics.RpcErrorTypes
files.Summary: DotBoxD-defined remote error type names.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Diagnostics.RpcHostErrorEventArgs
files.Summary: Describes a non-cancellation error observed by an accept loop.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Diagnostics.RpcPeerEventArgs
files.Summary: Describes an accepted connection.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Diagnostics.RpcProtocolErrorEventArgs
files.Summary: Describes a malformed or unsupported protocol frame observed by an .

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Diagnostics.RpcReadErrorEventArgs
files.Summary: Describes a non-cancellation failure in a 's read loop.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Exceptions.RemoteServiceException
files.Summary: Exception thrown when a remote RPC call fails.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Exceptions.ServiceConnectionException
files.Summary: Exception thrown when a connection fails.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Exceptions.ServiceException
files.Summary: Base exception for DotBoxD errors.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Exceptions.ServiceNotFoundException.NotFoundKind
files.Summary: Distinguishes which lookup produced the not-found result.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Exceptions.ServiceNotFoundException
files.Summary: Exception thrown when a service, method, or sub-service instance is not found.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Exceptions.ServiceProtocolException
files.Summary: Exception thrown when an inbound DotBoxD frame is malformed or cannot be decoded.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Exceptions.ServiceTimeoutException
files.Summary: Exception thrown when a request times out.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Generated.GeneratedMethod
files.Summary: Describes a source-generated DotBoxD service method.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Generated.GeneratedParameter
files.Summary: Describes a source-generated DotBoxD service method parameter.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Generated.GeneratedReturnKind
files.Summary: Classifies the generated RPC-facing return shape of a service method.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Generated.GeneratedService
files.Summary: Describes a source-generated DotBoxD service and its generated implementation types.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Generated.GeneratedServiceRegistry
files.Summary: Runtime registry populated by DotBoxD-generated service factories.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Generated.IDotBoxDGeneratedServiceRegistrationSink
files.Summary: Receives source-generated service, proxy, and dispatcher registrations without scanning generated types.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Generated.IDotBoxDServiceRegistrationSink
files.Summary: Receives source-generated service registrations without scanning generated types.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Peer.RpcPeer
files.Summary: One symmetric side of a DotBoxD connection. A peer can provide local services and get proxies for remote services over one demuxed read loop.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Peer.RpcPeerOptions
files.Summary: Options for and .

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Peer.RpcPeerSession
files.Summary: Owns a connected transport and the running over it. Dispose the session to close both in the correct order.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Protocol.MessageFramer.FramedMessage
files.Summary: A framed message read from a stream by . is the message body only — the 9-byte frame header has already been stripped, unlike the full-frame payload an IRpcChannel.ReceiveAsync returns. The caller owns it and must dispose it.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Protocol.MessageFramer
files.Summary: Handles message framing for the DotBoxD protocol. Stream frame: [4 bytes: Total Length][4 bytes: MessageId][1 byte: MessageType][N bytes: Body]. For RPC messages the body is un-nested as [4 bytes: Envelope Length][E bytes: Envelope][P bytes: Payload], so the trailing payload can be handed to callers as a zero-copy slice of the frame buffer.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Protocol.MessageType
files.Summary: Defines the types of messages in the DotBoxD protocol.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Protocol.RpcRequest
files.Summary: Represents an RPC request message. A struct so that framing a request does not heap-allocate the envelope on the client hot path; it is constructed, serialized, and read but never mutated after construction.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Protocol.RpcResponse
files.Summary: Represents an RPC response message. A struct so that framing a response does not heap-allocate the envelope on the server hot path; it is constructed, serialized, and read but never mutated after construction.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Protocol.RpcStreamHandle
files.Summary: Identifies a stream multiplexed over the current DotBoxD connection.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Protocol.RpcStreamKind
files.Summary: Describes the wire format carried by a DotBoxD stream.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Protocol.ServiceHandle
files.Summary: Wire payload returned by a service method whose declared return type is itself a [DotBoxDService] interface. Carries the opaque instance identifier the server allocated when the root method ran; the client wraps the handle in a generated sub-service proxy that uses the identifier on every subsequent call.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Serialization.ISerializer
files.Summary: Abstraction for message serialization.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Serialization.SerializerExtensions
files.Summary: Helpers for serializing directly into pooled buffers.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Server.IInstanceRegistry
files.Summary: Per-connection registry that holds server-side sub-service instances by opaque instance identifier. Created by the server when a connection is accepted and drained when the connection closes — instances therefore have connection-scoped lifetime and cannot leak across tenants.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Server.INonStreamingServiceDispatcher
files.Summary: Marker for dispatchers whose methods cannot consume streamed arguments or produce streamed responses. The peer can use this to skip allocating a streaming context for ordinary RPC calls.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Server.IRpcInvoker
files.Summary: The call surface a generated proxy uses to invoke methods the other side provides. Implemented by . This is the transport-agnostic invoke contract with no notion of "client" or "connect" — a peer simply forwards calls.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Server.IServiceDispatcher
files.Summary: Interface for generated service dispatchers that route incoming requests to the appropriate service method. Root-service dispatchers use DispatchAsync; sub-service dispatchers also implement DispatchOnInstanceAsync to route calls to a particular registered instance.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Server.InstanceRegistry
files.Summary: Default . Backed by a single keyed on (serviceName, instanceId). One registry per connection.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Server.RpcHost
files.Summary: Accepts connections from a listener and turns each one into an . The accept loop that used to live inside the server now lives here, and its output is peers: because each connection is a full peer, a host can both provide services to and call back into the peers that connect to it.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Streaming.Frames.RpcStreamAttachment
files.Summary: A local source that will be streamed over an RPC request or response.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Streaming.Remote.IRpcStreamingContext
files.Summary: Gives generated dispatchers access to streamed arguments and streamed responses.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Streaming.Remote.RpcStreamingContext
files.Summary: Runtime implementation of .

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Transport.IRpcChannel
files.Summary: A duplex, framed, bidirectional channel — the transport unit a runs on. Responses flow back over the same channel, so it is always bidirectional even when the call direction is one-way. Transports return this directly; implement it to add a custom transport.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Transport.IRpcFrameChannel
files.Summary: Optional low-allocation transport contract for channels that can transfer ownership of complete pooled frames instead of copying them into a new .

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Transport.IRpcValueTaskChannel
files.Summary: Optional low-allocation transport contract for channels that can complete send/receive operations without allocating a for each frame.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Transport.IServerTransport
files.Summary: Represents a server-side transport that accepts incoming connections.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Transport.ITransport
files.Summary: Represents a transport layer for establishing connections.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Transport.QueueFullMode
files.Summary: Controls what a bounded DotBoxD queue does when it is full.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Transport.RpcFrame
files.Summary:
Owns one received wire frame. The frame can be backed by the legacy owner or by a pooled writer transferred by a low-allocation transport.

This is a value type and may be copied. Ownership of the underlying or transfers only through ; otherwise callers dispose it through . Follow the boolean contract returned by RpcPeerFrameProcessor.ShouldDisposeAsync inside RpcPeerReadLoop: true means the caller owns the frame, false means the read loop retained ownership (for example, a StreamItem). Both backing owners are idempotent, so double-dispose is safe.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Transport.SingleConnectionServerTransport
files.Summary: Server transport that accepts one already-established connection.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Transport.SingleConnectionTransport
files.Summary: Client transport over an already-established connection.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Services.Transport.StreamConnection
files.Summary: DotBoxD connection over a duplex stream, including named pipe streams.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Transports.NamedPipes.NamedPipeClientTransport
files.Summary: Client transport for connecting to a DotBoxD server over a named pipe.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Transports.NamedPipes.NamedPipeServerTransport
files.Summary: Server transport for accepting DotBoxD connections over a named pipe.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Transports.Tcp.TcpConnection
files.Summary: TCP-based connection implementation.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Transports.Tcp.TcpServerTransport
files.Summary: TCP server transport implementation.

files.Title: DotBoxD.Transports.Tcp.TcpTransport
files.Summary: TCP client transport implementation.
